article_title: Rolls-Royce SMR Signs Czech Republic Early Works Contract With ČEZ for Temelín, Cementing Status as Europe's Only Multi-Country SMR Vendor

findings.message: Source redirected to different domain: abcnews.com

content_preview.title: Rolls-Royce SMR Signs Czech Republic Early Works Contract With ČEZ for Temelín, Cementing Status as Europe's Only Multi-Country SMR Vendor
content_preview.summary: Rolls-Royce SMR and Czech utility ČEZ signed an early works contract on April 24 to begin design, licensing and site work for the country's first small modular reactor at Temelín, targeting up to 3 GW of capacity and grid connection in the late 2030s.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Czech state-controlled utility ČEZ Group and Rolls-Royce SMR signed an early works contract in Prague on April 24, 2026, formally beginning preparatory work to deploy the United Kingdom company's small modular reactor at the existing Temelín nuclear power plant near the town of Týn nad Vltavou, [the Associated Press reported](https://abcnews.go.com/Technology/wireStory/czech-power-company-ez-signs-deal-rolls-royce-132339038). The agreement covers design, licensing documentation, env...

editor_notes.content_quality: Strong Energy News piece (~898 words). All 7 prior findings cleanly addressed.
editor_notes.source_verification: Re-verified all rewrite fixes. Source-3 (gov.uk GDA Step 2 — new) verifies verbatim 'pressurised water reactor (PWR) which is capable of generating 470 megawatts of electricity'. Source-0 (AP) confirms 'Tyn nad Vltavou' as the Temelin site location. Source-1 (gov.uk) and Source-4 (gov.uk selection — new) corroborate the June 2025 selection and 2.6 billion pound allocation. Source-2 (CNBC) confirms 'two-year selection process'. Wylfa generalized; Beneš converted to indirect; mid-decade framing dropped; mid-2030s grid connection moved to CNBC.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All 7 prior findings resolved. Sources expanded from 3 to 5 with two gov.uk pages added.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Clean APPROVE on re-review. Ready to merge.
